source_preflight.detail: Mermaid parses (graph LR). 7 nodes + 1 decision diamond, labels concise (node names + one clarifying sub-line each). Edges: CSV->READ->VAL, VAL-pass->VALID->CLEAN, VAL-fail->ERR->REPORT, plus dotted READ self-loop. Node count within guide.

concept_fidelity.detail: Matches corpus §5-§6 exactly: DictReader row-by-row, validate_row (presence/type/range), the valid/invalid two-bucket split, DictWriter clean output, and skip-and-log 'loop continues' semantics. The pass path is green, the fail path red — mirroring the corpus's valid vs invalid framing.

visual_inspection.detail: Rendered PNG (58 KB, 1600px) inspected. Clean left-to-right pipeline, decision diamond centered, two color-coded output paths (green pass -> valid/clean, red fail -> errors/report). Dotted self-loop labelled 'loop continues, never crashes'. No node overlaps, no label truncation, all text legible. Blue=source, purple=process, green=good bucket, red=bad bucket reads clearly.

verdict: PASS — source + visual both clean. LR data-flow pipeline foregrounds the two-bucket split (green valid path / red skip-and-log path) that is the topic's core idea. PNG rendered via mmdc + chrome-headless-shell override.
